keyTerms.Warwick Castle: A thousand-year-old fortification in central England, originally built in 1068 and continuously occupied for centuries
keyTerms.conservation: The practice of preserving historical structures by stabilizing materials and preventing decay rather than restoring to a 'new' state
keyTerms.Guy of Warwick: A legendary medieval giant-slayer whose story was the popular entertainment of its day, akin to modern superhero franchises
keyTerms.Greville family: The aristocratic family that owned Warwick Castle from 1604 to 1978, converting it from fortress to stately home
keyTerms.mortar: The binding material between stones — an anonymous, thousand-year-old decision by an unknown builder that still holds
